narrator.personality: Senior SRE. Dry, precise. Has seen intermittent failures before and hates them. States error rates to two decimal places.

story_beats.message: Error rate is holding at 31.2% for the last six hours. No pattern by time of day. No pattern by request payload. Same request succeeds, then fails, then succeeds again.
Claims processing SLA breach in 45 minutes. 847 claims queued behind the failures. Adjusters are processing manually as backup.
The governance team confirmed the SCP change went in last Tuesday. They say it should not affect Bedrock. They are wrong about many things.
The IAM role permissions are correct. The Bedrock model access is enabled. The intermittent pattern is the key -- what causes a service call to succeed sometimes and fail others with the same inputs?

glossary.Cross-Region Inference Profile: A Bedrock configuration that routes model requests across multiple AWS regions for load balancing and higher throughput. System-defined profiles use a fixed set of regions. The caller does not choose which region handles a given request -- Bedrock's internal load balancer decides.
glossary.Service Control Policy (SCP): A policy set at the AWS Organizations level that defines the maximum permissions for all accounts in an organizational unit. SCPs cannot grant access -- they can only restrict it. If an SCP denies an action, no IAM policy in the child account can override it.
glossary.aws:RequestedRegion: A global IAM condition key that evaluates to the AWS region where an API call is being made. SCPs commonly use this key with StringNotEquals to deny actions in unapproved regions.
glossary.Organizational Unit (OU): A grouping of AWS accounts within AWS Organizations. Policies like SCPs can be attached to an OU, and every account inside that OU inherits those restrictions.
glossary.AccessDeniedException: An error returned when an AWS API call is blocked by IAM, SCP, or resource policies. The error message sometimes specifies 'explicit deny in a service control policy,' which means an SCP is the source of the block.
glossary.Application Inference Profile: A user-created Bedrock inference profile that allows custom region selection. Unlike system-defined profiles, you control exactly which regions are included, so you can exclude regions blocked by organizational policies.
glossary.InvokeModel: The Bedrock API action used to send a prompt to a foundation model and receive a response. When called with a cross-region inference profile, the request may be routed to any region listed in that profile.
glossary.Load Balancer Routing: The mechanism by which a service distributes requests across multiple destinations. In Bedrock cross-region inference, routing is heuristics-based -- requests are spread across destination regions for throughput and availability, with no caller control over which region is selected.

narrative_arc.call: The error rate is 31.2%. It has been 31.2% for six hours. At Polaris Underwriting, one in three AI claim assessments is failing with AccessDeniedException. Eight hundred forty-seven claims are queued. The SLA clock is running.
narrative_arc.threshold: The IAM role is correct. The model access is enabled. The Lambda code has not changed. The same request succeeds, then fails, then succeeds again. The usual explanations do not apply. The problem is not in the application.
narrative_arc.trials: The engineer checks IAM policies, Bedrock model configuration, Lambda execution logs, and CloudWatch metrics. Everything that should matter is correct. The 31.2% error rate holds steady like a physical constant. The pattern is not in the payload, the timestamp, or the caller. It is somewhere else entirely.
narrative_arc.revelation: The cross-region inference profile routes to three regions. The SCP blocks one of those three. When Bedrock's load balancer picks the blocked region, the call dies. When it picks an allowed region, the call succeeds. One-third blocked. 31.2% failures. The SCP and the inference profile are operating at different layers and neither knows the other exists.
narrative_arc.return: The team creates an application inference profile that includes only the two allowed regions. The error rate drops to zero. A coordination process is established between the governance team and the engineering teams so that SCP changes are cross-referenced against active cross-region configurations before deployment.

components.name: Amazon SQS (claims-intake-q)
components.role: Receives insurance claim documents from the intake system and triggers the Lambda processor
components.failure_impact: If the queue is inaccessible, claims stop entering the pipeline entirely

components.name: Lambda (claims-processor)
components.role: Reads claims from SQS, sends them to Bedrock for AI assessment via the cross-region inference profile, and returns results to adjusters
components.failure_impact: If the function errors, individual claims fail and retry up to three times before landing in the dead-letter queue

components.name: IAM Role (polaris-claims-exec)
components.role: Grants the Lambda function permission to call bedrock:InvokeModel on the inference profile and underlying foundation models in all three regions
components.failure_impact: If the IAM policy is too narrow, all Bedrock calls fail consistently -- not intermittently

components.name: Amazon Bedrock (cross-region inference profile)
components.role: Routes InvokeModel requests across us-east-1, us-east-2, and us-west-2 using internal load balancing for throughput and availability
components.failure_impact: If one destination region is blocked at the organizational policy level, requests routed there fail while requests to allowed regions succeed -- producing an intermittent failure pattern

components.name: SCP (RegionRestriction-v3)
components.role: Denies all API actions (except IAM, Organizations, STS, Support, Budgets) in any region outside us-east-1 and us-west-2, applied to the Polaris OU
connections: All API calls in Polaris OU accounts
components.failure_impact: Any Bedrock request routed to us-east-2 is denied before reaching the Bedrock service endpoint, returning AccessDeniedException

components.name: AWS Organizations (Polaris OU)
components.role: Groups the Polaris Underwriting production and dev accounts under Meridian Insurance Group, inheriting all attached SCPs
components.failure_impact: If an SCP is too broad, it can silently break cross-region service features without any deployment-time validation

system_narration.data_flow: Claims arrive in the SQS queue. Lambda picks up each claim, assumes the polaris-claims-exec role, and calls bedrock:InvokeModel using the system-defined cross-region inference profile us.anthropic.claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022-v2:0. Bedrock's load balancer selects one of three destination regions (us-east-1, us-east-2, us-west-2) and routes the request there. Before the request reaches the Bedrock endpoint in the destination region, AWS Organizations evaluates the SCP attached to the Polaris OU. If the destination region is us-east-1 or us-west-2, the SCP allows the call and it succeeds. If the destination is us-east-2, the SCP denies the call and returns AccessDeniedException.
system_narration.what_broke: On Tuesday, the Meridian governance team applied RegionRestriction-v3 to the Polaris OU, removing us-east-2 from the approved region list. The cross-region inference profile still includes us-east-2 as a destination. When Bedrock's load balancer routes a request to us-east-2, the SCP intercepts and denies it. Roughly one-third of all requests are routed there, producing a stable 31.2% failure rate. The SCP and the inference profile operate at different layers -- Organizations-level access control and Bedrock-level routing -- and neither validates against the other.

hints.text: The failures are not random. They follow a pattern, but the pattern is not in the request. It is in the routing.
This account uses a cross-region inference profile. Where can Bedrock route the requests?
Check which regions the inference profile includes. Now check which regions the SCP allows.
The SCP blocks us-east-2. The inference profile includes us-east-2. When Bedrock routes there, the call is denied. When it routes to allowed regions, it succeeds.

resolution.root_cause: A cross-region inference profile routes Bedrock InvokeModel calls across us-east-1, us-east-2, and us-west-2 for load balancing. A Service Control Policy applied by the parent organization blocks all API calls in us-east-2. When Bedrock routes to us-east-2, the call fails with AccessDeniedException. When it routes to an allowed region, it succeeds, producing an intermittent ~30% failure rate.

fix_criteria.description: Player identifies that the SCP blocks API calls in us-east-2
Player identifies that the cross-region inference profile includes us-east-2 as a destination region
Player connects the intermittent failure pattern to Bedrock's load-balancing across regions, some of which are SCP-blocked
Player proposes either updating the SCP to allow Bedrock in us-east-2 or switching to a geographic inference profile that only uses allowed regions

learning_objectives: How cross-region inference profiles in Amazon Bedrock route requests across multiple regions for load balancing
How Service Control Policies interact with cross-region AWS service features
Debugging intermittent failures by examining the routing layer rather than the application layer
Coordinating cloud governance policies (SCPs) with service-level configurations (inference profiles)
sop_steps: 1. Acknowledge the incident alert and confirm the 31.2% error rate on Bedrock InvokeModel calls in CloudWatch metrics.
2. Pull the last 50 invocation log entries and confirm the failing requests all return AccessDeniedException with 'explicit deny in a service control policy.'
3. Note that the error messages reference the us-east-2 Bedrock endpoint ARN -- the calls are being denied in that specific region.
4. Describe the cross-region inference profile us.anthropic.claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022-v2:0 and list its destination regions: us-east-1, us-east-2, us-west-2.
5. List SCPs attached to the Polaris OU using organizations:ListPoliciesForTarget. Identify RegionRestriction-v3.
6. Describe the SCP content. Confirm it denies all actions (except IAM, Organizations, STS, Support, Budgets) in regions outside us-east-1 and us-west-2. The region us-east-2 is blocked.
7. Correlate: the inference profile routes to three regions, the SCP allows two of three. One-third of requests hit the blocked region and fail.
8. Immediate mitigation: switch the Lambda function to invoke the model directly in us-east-1 (single-region), eliminating cross-region routing to blocked regions.
9. Short-term fix: create an application inference profile using CreateInferenceProfile that includes only us-east-1 and us-west-2. Update the Lambda to use the new profile ARN.
10. Verify the error rate drops to baseline (<0.3%) by monitoring CloudWatch InvocationErrors for 15 minutes.
11. Long-term: coordinate with the Meridian governance team to add a pre-deployment check that cross-references SCP region restrictions against active cross-region inference profile configurations.
12. Document the incident timeline, root cause, and remediation in the post-incident review.

related_failure_modes.scenario: SCP blocks all Bedrock actions, producing 100% failure instead of intermittent
related_failure_modes.how_it_differs: If the SCP denied bedrock:InvokeModel in all regions (including the source region), every single request would fail -- not just one-third. The error rate would be 100% instead of 31.2%, and the pattern would be obvious rather than puzzling. The intermittent nature of this incident is what makes it harder to diagnose.
related_failure_modes.prevention: When applying region-restriction SCPs, enumerate all cross-region AWS features in use (inference profiles, S3 replication, DynamoDB global tables) and verify every destination region is in the approved list.

related_failure_modes.scenario: IAM policy missing permissions for one destination region in the inference profile
related_failure_modes.how_it_differs: If the IAM policy on the Lambda role only granted bedrock:InvokeModel for models in us-east-1 and us-west-2 but not us-east-2, the symptom would be similar -- intermittent AccessDeniedException. However, the error message would say 'not authorized to perform' without mentioning a service control policy. The fix would be updating the IAM policy resource ARNs rather than the SCP or inference profile.
related_failure_modes.prevention: When configuring IAM policies for cross-region inference, include foundation model ARNs for every region listed in the inference profile. Use the bedrock:InferenceProfileArn condition key to scope access precisely.

related_failure_modes.scenario: Bedrock destination region is an opt-in region that was never enabled
related_failure_modes.how_it_differs: Some AWS regions require explicit opt-in at the account level. If a cross-region inference profile includes an opt-in region that the account has not enabled, requests routed there fail. The error may differ from an SCP deny -- it could appear as a region-not-enabled error rather than AccessDeniedException. The fix is to enable the region in account settings rather than changing SCPs.
related_failure_modes.prevention: Before using cross-region inference profiles, confirm all destination regions are enabled in the account. Check the inference profile documentation for which regions are opt-in.

related_failure_modes.scenario: AWS Control Tower region deny control blocks cross-region inference
related_failure_modes.how_it_differs: Organizations using AWS Control Tower may have a landing zone region deny control that functions similarly to a manual SCP but is managed by Control Tower. The symptoms are identical, but the remediation path differs -- you must modify the Control Tower configuration or add a targeted SCP exception rather than editing the region deny control directly.
related_failure_modes.prevention: When enabling cross-region inference in a Control Tower environment, use the built-in mechanisms to extend region deny controls with Bedrock-specific exceptions, as described in the AWS documentation for enabling cross-region inference in multi-account environments.

sop_practices: Before applying SCP region restrictions, inventory all cross-region AWS features in active use -- inference profiles, S3 cross-region replication, DynamoDB global tables, Aurora global databases -- and verify every destination region remains in the approved list.
Use application inference profiles instead of system-defined profiles when operating under SCPs, so you control exactly which regions are included and can align them with organizational policy.
Add a pre-deployment validation step to the governance pipeline that cross-references proposed SCP region changes against active cross-region service configurations in affected accounts.

Monitor Bedrock InvocationErrors as a percentage of InvocationCount with a CloudWatch alarm. A stable, non-trivial error rate (not near 0% or 100%) is a strong signal of a routing or policy conflict rather than an application bug.
